content.problemStatement: Q4) A . Differentiate between structure and union ?   ---3 Marks.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 5), the number of questions. Each of the next T lines contains a question string (length ≤ 200).
content.outputFormat:
For each question, output exactly two lines:
1. "A structure is a collection of variables of different data types that are stored together in memory."
2. "A union is a special type of structure where all the members share the same memory space."

sampleTestCases.input:
2
What is the difference between structure and union?
Explain structure vs union.
sampleTestCases.output:
A structure is a collection of variables of different data types that are stored together in memory.
A union is a special type of structure where all the members share the same memory space.
A structure is a collection of variables of different data types that are stored together in memory.
A union is a special type of structure where all the members share the same memory space.
sampleTestCases.explanation: T=2, so the two fixed lines are printed twice, once for each question.

sampleTestCases.output:

A union is a special type of structure where all the members share the same memory space.
sampleTestCases.explanation: Single question yields exactly the two required lines.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times, reading and discarding each question line (it is irrelevant for the answer). After reading a line, print the two fixed explanation sentences. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time operation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of T.

code.Python3:
import sys
lines = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
if not lines:
    sys.exit()
T = int(lines[0])
line1 = "A structure is a collection of variables of different data types that are stored together in memory."
line2 = "A union is a special type of structure where all the members share the same memory space."
out = []
for i in range(1, T + 1):
    # question = lines[i]  # ignored
    out.append(line1)
    out.append(line2)
sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
